info.description:
REST API for the River self-hosted media platform.
Auth flow: POST /api/auth/login → use the returned
access_token as a Bearer token on the protected routes.
The stream_token is a longer-lived JWT restricted to
/stream and /download endpoints (the <video> element
can't send custom auth headers; it goes in the URL as
?token=…).
info.title: River API

post.description: Public endpoint. The first registered user becomes admin.

parameters.description: Access JWT (also accepted via Authorization header)
parameters.name: token

parameters.description: Also remove show folder on disk
parameters.name: delete_files

parameters.description: Access JWT (also accepted via Authorization header)
parameters.name: token

stream_index.description: 0-based audio stream index in the video file

source_path.description:
SourcePath is the original on-disk location of this episode before
transcoding/copying. Stream/download fall back to it when FilePath
isn't yet pointing at a real file (transcode hasn't finished). See
the matching field on Movie for the full rationale.

SourcePath is the original on-disk location the scanner discovered
the movie at (typically under MEDIA_PATH). FilePath is the canonical
post-transcode/post-copy location (typically under OUTPUT_PATH).
Stream/download endpoints prefer FilePath but fall back to SourcePath
when FilePath is empty or the file isn't on disk yet — this lets the
UI offer playback before the transcode/copy step has finished.

folder_path.description:
FolderPath is the show's root directory on disk, recorded by
river-scan when it first discovers (or resolves) the show. The admin
"identify" flow uses this to trigger a targeted re-scan that picks
up episodes added after the initial scan.

BearerAuth.description: Bearer JWT — paste "Bearer <token>" or use the Authorize button.
BearerAuth.name: Authorization
